weekly.name: Allie
weekly.bio:
Allie Muzzio is a licensed Occupational Therapist and registered yoga teacher, completing both the 200-Hour Yoga Teacher Training and Kids Yoga Teacher Training at Sama Academy. She holds a Master's degree in Occupational Therapy with a minor in Psychology, integrating evidence-based OT principles with nervous system support, functional movement, and embodied awareness.

With specialized training in Pediatric Yoga & Mindfulness, Allie guides students of all ages in developing tools for safety, confidence, and self-regulation—both on and off the mat. Her approach blends therapeutic insight with accessible movement and breath awareness, helping students meet themselves with curiosity and kindness. She looks forward to supporting you here at Sama!

weekly.name: Amber
weekly.bio: Amber is thrilled to be with you. She is a New Canaan resident, native of the Netherlands, mom of 3, avid learner and proud graduate of Sama Academy's 200 hr Yoga Teacher Training Program. Amber's signature classes offer steady and easeful sequences to set you on your wonderful day ahead. Welcome to Sama!

weekly.name: Amy
weekly.bio:
Amy is a leader in our community, serving as art educator and lead at a private school in Greenwich. She is a graduate of Sama Yoga Academy's YTT-200-hour and Restorative Teacher Training programs and a mentor for Sama Academy. Amy's experience and steady presence guide us into harmony.

In 2020 Amy founded bilden farmacy, hand-poured candles available here at Sama that offer rituals from the mat to your home. She is an artist, art educator and marathon runner and can not wait to welcome you.

weekly.name: Denise
weekly.bio:
After a career in Finance and 10 years abroad, Denise and her family settled in Darien, CT. She was an avid Sama student and developed into a proud graduate of Sama Yoga Academy's 200 Hour YTT, Sama's 30 Hour Kids and Sama's 25 Hour Restorative YTT. She dedicates some of her time to volunteering including the Norwalk Grassroots Tennis & Education Center.

She loves her family dog walks, neighborhood runs, friendly tennis matches and time on her mat. Denise believes that one secret to maintaining an active lifestyle is finding a little time to slow down. She invites you to come to your mat, take a breath and move mindfully so you are energized ready for your day.

weekly.name: Laura
weekly.bio:
Laura is an RYT 200 Yoga Instructor, Sama Academy Graduate, Certified Personal Trainer, originally from the UK, a former finance professional, and a mother of four. She is a lifelong lover of movement, actively engaged in racquet sports, running, and strength training. She teaches through the lens of balance, strength and softness, effort and ease, flow and release.

Her yoga practice began as support for an active lifestyle, but the grounding breathwork and meditative focus soon became essential to calming her nervous system.

In class, she offers both challenge and space to soften, guiding students to explore strength, release, and the place where they meet.

weekly.name: Mary
weekly.bio:
Mary has been practicing yoga for over fifteen years, and is proud to be a graduate of Sama Academy's Yoga Teacher Training. She is mother of six-year-old twins.

She currently leads partnerships for Thirty Madison, a platform increasing access to virtual specialized healthcare. Prior to Thirty Madison, Mary led strategy and growth initiatives for the non-profit DoSomething.org, as well as at mission-driven tech startups Andela and ZocDoc. Mary graduated Magna Cum Laude from Fordham University, where she studied International Political Economy and Peace & Justice Studies.

weekly.name: Sarah
weekly.bio:
Sarah began practicing yoga over 7 years ago in search of balance, harmony, and inner strength, and immediately fell in love. As a graduate of Sama Yoga Academy's 200-hour Yoga Teacher Training, she seeks to offer the support and steady easefulness that yoga has brought to her life in every one of her classes.

Outside the studio, Sarah bounces between Norwalk and New York City working in book publishing and fills her free time with long runs, crochet projects, and matcha lattes. She is a cat mom and dog walking volunteer at a local shelter and will always say yes to seeing a picture of your pet.

Sarah invites you to leave your day at the door and join her on the mat for an hour of blissful unplugging, joyful movement, and the occasional fiery flow.

weekly.name: Tamara
weekly.bio:
Tamara is honored to have completed Sama's 200-hour YTT and Restorative Yoga Program has been practicing yoga for over 30 years. With a background in personal training and group fitness, she has explored many movement modalities—but yoga has always been her true home.

She is passionate about helping others feel strong, supported, and empowered at every stage of life. Tamara is excited to share the joy, curiosity, and connection that yoga brings—both on and off the mat. Welcome!

weekly.name: Zach
weekly.bio: Graduate of Sama Academy's YTT-200-hour and Children's Teacher Training. With a Masters in educational psychology and undergrad in Human Development and Family Science, Zach is passionate about every human being being heard and seen, He is a graduate of Sama Yoga Academy's 200 hr YTT program and is currently an elementary school teacher in Bridgeport, CT. Through his mindful flows and innate kindness, Zach shares that he started yoga as an adult and is eager to meet you where you are.

substitutes.name: Anna
substitutes.bio:
Anna earned her RYT-200 hour teaching certification from Sama Yoga Academy's Teacher Training. On the side, she holds an MBA, a B.S. in Marketing and a B.A. in German; Anna works in consumer behavior and branding functions.

Anna's upbeat spirit and classes lift us up! "Movement, mindfulness, strength, and stretch are Sama's recipe for joy!" Join her in flow!

substitutes.name: April
substitutes.bio:
April has been practicing yoga for over 15 years. A lifelong athlete, her relationship with movement transformed after being diagnosed with fibromyalgia at a young age, followed by chronic Lyme disease. Through these experiences, she was invited to slow down to cultivate a compassionate, intuitive understanding of her body.

April first came to Sama drawn to its belief that yoga is truly for every body, and is a proud graduate of the Sama Yoga Center Teacher Training program. Her teaching is rooted in that same philosophy—creating a space where students feel seen, supported, and empowered to meet themselves exactly as they are. She is passionate about helping others discover that strength, healing, and connection are always available, no matter where they begin.

substitutes.name: Caroline
substitutes.bio:
Caroline is passionate about the benefits of slowing down and connecting to inner calm through the practice of yoga. She holds a B.S. in Educational Studies from Colgate University and a MAT in Early Childhood Education from Manhattanville College. In 2019, she completed her 200-hour YTT.

Here at Sama, she obtained her Kids Yoga Certification and Restorative Yoga Certification. Caroline is a Grade 3 teacher at New Canaan Country School and the Director of the Sama Kids Program.

substitutes.name: Heather
substitutes.bio: With three children and a Darien, CT native, Heather understands the pressures and joys of managing our time and priorities. As a proud graduate of Sama Yoga Academy's Teacher Training Program, she has taken yoga from passion to priority. She is always a student and here to support you. "Welcome – may you be comfortable, safe and flexible in mind and body on your mat and into your day."

substitutes.name: Megan
substitutes.bio: Megan Yee is a psychotherapist and a graduate of Sama Yoga Academy's 200-hour YTT, who brings depth, creativity, and presence to her teaching. A lifelong violinist and community orchestra leader, she offers moving meditation with live music to wellness spaces across Fairfield County, exploring the relationship between sound, movement, and emotional well-being. She also facilitates hands-on craft workshops and enjoys making whimsical art, viewing creativity as a powerful pathway to healing and self-expression. Community and connection are at the heart of all that Megan does.

substitutes.name: Missy
substitutes.bio:
Missy has been practicing yoga since her 20's, achieved her goal to become a teacher and is a graduate of Sama Yoga Academy's 200 Hour YTT. She balances her time being both a Digital Ad Agency executive and a yoga teacher.

Missy lives in Ridgefield with her two teenage daughters, English husband and two dogs. She is passionate about yoga, tennis, pickleball and refining her sourdough skills! Her classes guide us to build strength, awareness and harmony from our bones to our brain.

substitutes.name: Molly
substitutes.bio: Molly has been a student of yoga for over 10 years and is grateful to have the opportunity to graduate from Sama's 200 hr YTT Program. In Molly's view, yoga is the quintessential therapy for mental and physical health and helps her navigate the challenges of her corporate responsibilities with steadiness and perspective. Molly can't wait to share the light, love and joy of yoga through uplifting and energizing practices with you here at Sama. Welcome!

substitutes.name: Sara
substitutes.bio: Sara is a proud graduate of Sama Yoga Academy's 200 hr YTT, and Savannah College of Art and Design with a major in fashion design. She works as a sewing and crafting teacher in Darien and Greenwich, designs clothes, knits and crochets in her free time.
